# Dashdown

> Dashdown turns Markdown files — with embedded SQL and component tags — into interactive analytics dashboards.

Dashdown renders Markdown (with embedded SQL and `<Component />` tags) as interactive analytics dashboards. The links below are the documentation, one page per topic. For the entire manual in a single file, fetch `/llms-full.txt`.

## Documentation

- [Getting started](/getting-started): Dashdown is published to PyPI as the `dashdown-md` package, with a CLI entry point named `dashdown`
- [Installation](/installation): Dashdown is published to PyPI as **`dashdown-md`**, and the CLI it installs is the command **`dashdown`**
- [Examples](/examples): Real, end-to-end dashboards built with Dashdown
- [Writing pages](/pages): Every file under `pages/` is a route
- [Configuration](/configuration): Every project has a `dashdown.yaml` at its root — the single config file for the whole dashboard
- [Connectors](/connectors): Connectors are declared in `sources.yaml` and loaded **lazily** the first time a query asks for that type
- [Queries](/queries): SQL lives either in the shared **query library** under `queries/` (the recommended default) or, for a quick one-off, inline in a named ` ```sql ` block on a…
- [Python queries](/python-queries): Some questions are awkward — or impossible — in a single SQL statement: a **forecast**, an ML score, a **cross-connector join**, an external-API pull, a…
- [Semantic layer](/semantic-layer): Define your metrics and dimensions **once**, then reference them straight from a component — no per-chart SQL, no copy-pasted queries:
- [Real-time data](/realtime): A query opts into **live streaming** with the `live` attribute: `interval=N` sets the poll cadence in seconds (default `5`, floored to `1`)
- [Components](/components): A component is a PascalCase tag you drop into a page
- [Filters & parameters](/filters): Filters write into a central reactive store; any query that references the filter's name with `${...}` re-runs when it changes
- [Formatting](/formatting): Tables, counters, values and chart axes all render numbers and dates through **one** formatter, so `63712.895` becomes `$63,712.90` the same way everywhere
- [Theming & styling](/theming): Dashdown ships a polished light/dark theme out of the box, but every project can override the look — colours, radii, spacing, chrome, typography — with **one…
- [Detail pages](/detail-pages): A **detail page** — a "drill-down" or sub page — is a single template that serves a whole collection: one file renders the focused view for *every* record,…
- [AI](/ai): Dashdown is built for AI in **two directions** — the LLM that helps your *readers* understand a dashboard, and the coding agent that helps *you* build one
- [Full-text search](/search): `<SiteSearch />` is a built-in component that searches **every page** of the project
- [Exporting](/exporting): Renders the project to a serverless static site through the *exact same* render path as the live server
- [Extending Dashdown](/extending): Dashdown has two extension points, both plain Python: **custom components** and **custom connectors**
- [CLI reference](/cli): Everything Dashdown does from the terminal goes through the `dashdown` command
- [Telemetry & privacy](/telemetry): Dashdown is pip-installed and self-hosted, with no accounts and no server in the loop

## Full text

- [Complete documentation](/llms-full.txt): every topic concatenated into one file.
